<tool_call>

<parameter=code>
# Check what changed
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

print("Current M squares:")
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] == 'M' and n['pixels'] == 4:
        print(f"  Node {n['id']}: boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Compare with previous frame

    prev_seg = previous_frame.segmentation
    prev_nodes = prev_seg['nodes']
    print("\nPrevious M squares:")
    for n in prev_nodes:

            print(f"  Node {n['id']}: boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Nothing changed! Clicking at [34, 38] didn't move Node 9. But Node 9 is at [33-34, 37-38], so [34, 38] is the bottom-right corner of Node 9. Maybe clicking on the bottom-right corner doesn't do anything.
Let me try clicking on the center of Node 9, which would be [33, 37] (top-left corner) or [34, 38] (bottom-right corner). Wait, for a 2×2 square at [33-34, 37-38], the center cells are [33, 37], [33, 38], [34, 37], [34, 38]. All 4 cells are part of the 2×2 square.
Hmm, but earlier I clicked at [34, 38] and it moved Node 9 up-left by 1. Let me check...
Actually, looking at the previous output, Node 9 was at [34-35, 38-39] before the click, and [33-34, 37-38] after the click. So clicking at [34, 38] did move Node 9 up-left by 1.
But now Node 9 is at [33-34, 37-38], and clicking at [34, 38] didn't move it. That's strange.
Wait, maybe the board didn't change because the action was executed but the result shows "board_changed": false. Let me check...
Actually, looking at the action result, it says "board_changed": true. But the segmentation shows no change. Let me re-check...
Hmm, the current and previous M squares are identical. So the board didn't change, but the action result says "board_changed": true. This is confusing.
Wait, maybe the action result is from a previous action, not the current one. Let me check the action result more carefully.
Actually, the action result shows "action_display": "MOUSE(row=34, col=38)" and "board_changed": true. But the segmentation shows no change. This is strange.
Let me try clicking on a different cell of Node 9, such as [33, 37].

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me try clicking on [33, 37] (top-left of Node 9)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 33, 'col': 37}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
